vulnerabilities.description: Insufficient token/port validation. Global variables __IMPECCABLE_TOKEN__ and __IMPECCABLE_PORT__ are checked for existence but not format/content validation. Attackers controlling global scope could inject malicious values. Token should be validated against expected format before use.

Weak session ID generation fallback. Uses Math.random().toString(16).slice(2, 10) as fallback instead of crypto.randomUUID(). Math.random() is not cryptographically secure and predictable session IDs could allow session hijacking.

Sensitive token exposed in global scope without XSS protection. Browser injection scripts accessing window.__IMPECCABLE_TOKEN__ are vulnerable to other XSS attacks stealing the token. Should use postMessage API or secure iframes instead.

CSS selector escaping without HTML sanitization. The cssId() function properly escapes CSS but DOM injection methods (uiAppend, uiAppendStyle) don't sanitize HTML content, risking XSS if user-controlled data flows through.

localStorage used without encryption for session state. Session data (including checkpointRevision, IDs) stored in plaintext localStorage. No protection if page is compromised. Consider indexedDB with encryption or server-side sessions.
Potential race condition in session corruption recovery. Atomic write fix (#2307) is implemented, but corrupted sessions are silently auto-recovered. Should log corruption incidents for monitoring.

recommendations: Implement strict validation for __IMPECCABLE_TOKEN__ format (whitelist alphanumeric, check length, reject suspicious patterns) before use
Replace Math.random() fallback with always using crypto.randomUUID() or remove fallback entirely with error handling
Use postMessage API for token passing between frames instead of exposing to global scope. Validate origin in message handlers
Add HTML sanitization layer (DOMPurify or similar) to DOM injection methods even if content is internal
Encrypt session data in localStorage using SubtleCrypto or move session state to server with secure HTTPOnly cookies
Add Content-Security-Policy headers: script-src 'self', connect-src 'self', frame-src 'self' to reduce injection surface
Implement CSRF token validation for the localhost:PORT injection endpoint
Audit and log all localStorage access and session state changes for security monitoring
Add timeout to manual session application state (MANUAL_APPLY_STATE_TTL_MS) to prevent stale token reuse
Use Subresource Integrity (SRI) hashes if importing external scripts like live.js
